mnemonics: STASH: Steganography, Tamper-proofing, Authentication, Security, Hiding
keyPoints: Data hiding is used for covert communication (steganography) to conceal the existence of a message.
It provides tamper-proofing by embedding digital watermarks to verify authenticity and integrity of data.
It enhances security through obscurity, adding a layer of protection by making critical information less obvious.
commonMistakes: Confusing data hiding (concealing existence) with encryption (scrambling content).
Assuming data hiding alone provides robust security, when it should be a complementary layer to encryption.
